routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 scramble via SW slopes/Cave Ridge; roped travel not standard. Some parties carry a 20-30m handline + prusik for a short exposed step near the summit ridge. Helmet strongly recommended for climber-induced rockfall in the talus field.

Rope up for crevassed Neve Glacier crossing (col near Neve Peak to Snowfield's west ridge); unrope for the Class 3 summit ridge scramble. Trip reports note some leaders carry a small cam/nut rack for a handline on the scramble.

Area ('Sofa King Buttress') not found on Mountain Project's Washington Pass sub-area index or other guidebook sources searched. Inferred from grade/regional style as a short bolted granite pitch typical of Washington Pass sport crags.
routes.corrections: Could not verify area name/location despite exhaustive search of Washington Pass's 14 indexed sub-areas; gear inferred, not fabricated from trip reports.

routes.rope_note: Same unverified area as Drilling Me Softly (Sofa King Buttress). Inferred bolted-sport gear from grade/regional style.
routes.corrections: Area not found on Mountain Project; see wa_drilling_me_softly note.

routes.rope_type: single (double ropes common for descent raps)
routes.rope_note: 3-pitch trad line on South Early Winters Spire's granite. Standard alpine rack to 3in, extra small cams for roof cracks. SEWS routes are commonly descended via multiple rappels down the SW Couloir, so many parties carry twin 60m ropes.

sling_rack.cams: single set 0.3-3in, doubles in finger/hand sizes

routes.rope_note: Classic 5.9+ chimney/face line, SEWS. Standard rack to 3in; wide piece (#3-#4) useful for the chimney section.
6-pitch 5.11- testpiece on SEWS; modern mixed bolt/gear protection typical of the harder Washington Pass free lines.
5.11b sport/trad hybrid pitch(es) on SEWS, same buttress as Free Mojo/Northwest Face; mixed protection.
6-pitch 5.11a/b Boving-Pollock line on SEWS's NW face; long multi-rappel descent makes twin 60m ropes the common choice.
Easiest/most popular SEWS line, mostly difficult scramble to low 5th (5.5); small light rack, often simul-climbed or short-roped by faster parties.
Sustained 9-pitch 5.11d testpiece on SEWS; full rack plus extra small cams for sustained crux pitches.
9-pitch 5.11- SEWS route; standard hard-free alpine rack, similar to Southern Man on the same wall.
Pinto Rock 'South Face' area, Beckey guidebook's 'regular route.' 400ft/4 pitches, knobby welded-tuff rock; mixed bolt + knob-thread protection.
Pinto Rock South Face, 5 pitches, knobby rock ('cobbletrust' skill-building route); mixed bolt + occasional gear placements.

Gunsight Range South Peak, remote granite spire reached via Chikamin Glacier. Not individually confirmed on MP; inferred consistent with the East Face/West Face naming pattern on neighboring Middle/North Peak.
routes.corrections: Route not individually listed in fetched MP data; grade/style inferred from area pattern.

routes.rope_note: Confirmed on Mountain Project: Gunsight Range South Peak, 5.8, 3 pitches. Remote glacier approach; trip reports cite twin 60m x 9mm ropes plus full snow/glacier/rock kit.
Gunsight Range South Peak. Not individually confirmed; inferred from area pattern and remote-glacier access logistics shared with South Ridge.
routes.corrections: Route not individually detailed in fetched MP data.

routes.rope_note: South Twin Sister (Twin Sisters Range), West Ridge. 2hrs of Class 2-3 scrambling on grippy olivine; staying on the ridge crest hits a couple of low-5th-class spots where roping up is sensible.

Southeast Mox Peak, 2,500ft East Face big-wall alpine route (23 pitches). First ascent placed no bolts and rappelled the route entirely on gear anchors; extremely remote/serious. Full trad rack plus abundant rap-anchor cord essential.

sling_rack.pitons: a few, optional, for old/thin placements

routes.rope_note: Entiat Mountains. West Ridge/West Gully line up; gentle Class 2-3 with a minor cliff band (~6,800ft) requiring routefinding but no rope.
routes.corrections: North vs South Spectacle Butte 'West Route' not fully disambiguated in sources.

routes.rope_note: Northern Pickets, remote south face/ridge route: ~800ft of runout climbing into a chimney, a rappel into a notch, then exposed face climbing to a gendarme.
Off-trail, densely forested Class 3 peak near Rt 2/PCT; no glaciers, no technical rope typically needed - mostly bushwhack and talus/scramble.
routes.corrections: Specific 'South Route' line not individually documented; grade/gear inferred from general peak description.

routes.rope_note: Spire Gully right - Alpenkuhl (Washington Pass bolted crag, 7min walk from hairpin). Confirmed on MP: 5.9, sport, single pitch.
Spire Gully right - Alpenkuhl. Confirmed on MP: 5.10, sport, single pitch.
Spire Gully right - Alpenkuhl. Confirmed on MP: 5.10 (task lists 5.10b), sport, single pitch, 3-star.
Spire Gully right - Alpenkuhl. Confirmed on MP: 5.8+, TRAD (only trad route in this otherwise-bolted cluster) - needs a real rack, not just draws.
Spire Gully right - Alpenkuhl. Confirmed on MP: 5.8, sport, single pitch, 2.5-star.
Spire Mountain near Index; ~6mi off-trail, 5,000ft gain, finishing on the west ridge. Mostly Class 3 with short Class 4/occasional Class 5 spots where a rope is sometimes used.
Remote Dome Peak-area summit; south/southwest face is a 5-pitch 5.6 (low-5th-class) rock route reached via glacier approach. Light trad rack plus glacier travel gear for approach.

Lake Chelan-Sawtooth Wilderness, via Fish Creek Pass. Class 2-3 scree/talus with easy scrambling; early-season parties carry an ice axe for a snow ramp below the summit ridge, otherwise non-technical.
Steeple Rock (Olympics basalt pillar, Hurricane Ridge). Easy 5th class (~5.0) scramble along the ridge crest to the summit block; short pitch, small rack.
Steeple Rock, 5.8 crack up the northernmost line on the west face, left-facing corner along a pillar to a belay stance atop it. Standard small rack.
Confirmed on MP: Guye Peak, 5.11b, 50ft sport/mixed pitch - bolts plus one #1 Camalot placement into a pod near the start; 2-bolt chain anchor, no top-out.

Confirmed on MP: Guye Peak west face, Grade III, 6 pitches, 800ft, 5.8, standard medium alpine rack, some fixed pro on the traverse pitch. IMPORTANT: a Nov 2021 rockfall destroyed part of the crux traverse (now 5.9-5.10 downclimbing with minimal pro through loose rock) - MP explicitly states 'Climbing this route is not recommended' as of its last update.
routes.corrections: Route is currently discouraged/hazardous per Mountain Project due to Nov 2021 rockfall damage to the crux traverse pitch.

routes.rope_note: Confirmed on MP: Guye Peak, Easy 5th class/Grade II. Small rock rack, single rope, slings for tree anchors.

Confirmed on MP: Guye Peak, Easy 5th class/Grade II, 800ft, 5 pitches. Single rack of cams .75-2in is sufficient; protection is intermittent/poor in dirty, flaring cracks, so bring extra slings to extend placements.

sling_rack.extra_slings: many, for extension due to poor rock/pro quality

routes.corrections: Task grade ('4th') is slightly easier than MP's listed 'Easy 5th class' - likely the same route, minor grade-convention discrepancy.
